2026-04-01T21:45:27.9418118Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-01T21:43:44.670663276Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57684,\"title\":\"New foundry *_call/*_call_output items can\\u0026#39;t be sent as Responses input; Conversations API returns undocumented remote_function_call instead\",\"body\":\"# [BUG] Foundry agent tool call types have dual discriminator and block stateless multi-turn replay\\n\\n## Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.AI.Projects 2.0.0-beta.2 (transitively pulls Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI 2.0.0-beta.1, OpenAI 2.9.1)\\n\\n## Describe the bug\\n\\nTwo related issues with how Azure-specific agent tool call types (e.g. `openapi_call`) surface through the Responses and Conversations APIs:\\n\\n### 1. Stateless multi-turn replay fails for Azure tool types\\n\\nWhen using `ProjectResponsesClient` with an agent that has Azure-specific tools (OpenAPI, AI Search, Bing Grounding), `ResponseResult.OutputItems` contains items with `\\u0026#34;type\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;openapi_call\\u0026#34;`. Attempting to replay these as `InputItems` in a follow-up request (stateless multi-turn) results in HTTP 400:\\n\\n```\\nHTTP 400 (invalid_request_error: invalid_value)\\nParameter: input[1]\\nInvalid value: \\u0026#39;openapi_call\\u0026#39;. Supported values are: \\u0026#39;apply_patch_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;apply_patch_call_output\\u0026#39;,\\n\\u0026#39;code_interpreter_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;compaction\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;computer_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;computer_call_output\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;custom_tool_call\\u0026#39;,\\n\\u0026#39;custom_tool_call_output\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;file_search_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;function_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;function_call_output\\u0026#39;,\\n\\u0026#39;image_generation_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;item_reference\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;local_shell_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;local_shell_call_output\\u0026#39;,\\n\\u0026#39;mcp_approval_request\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;mcp_approval_response\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;mcp_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;mcp_list_tools\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;message\\u0026#39;,\\n\\u0026#39;reasoning\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;shell_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;shell_call_output\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;tool_search_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;tool_search_output\\u0026#39;,\\nand \\u0026#39;web_search_call\\u0026#39;.\\n```\\n\\nThis forces users into server-managed state (`PreviousResponseId` or Conversations API) — there\\u0026#39;s no way to do stateless multi-turn with Azure-specific tools.\\n\\n### 2. Dual discriminator: Conversations API returns undocumented type\\n\\nThe same logical operation (an OpenAPI tool call) surfaces with different `type` values depending on the API:\\n\\n| API | JSON `type` value | SDK deserialization result |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Responses API `output[]` | `openapi_call` | [`InternalUnknownItemResource`](https://github.com/openai/openai-dotnet/blob/[REDACTED]/src/Generated/Models/Responses/ResponseItem.Serialization.cs#L158) (OpenAI SDK doesn\\u0026#39;t know it) |\\n| Conversations API items | `remote_function_call` (with `\\u0026#34;label\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;remote_openapi\\u0026#34;`) | [`UnknownAgentResponseItem`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/src/Generated/Models/AgentResponseItem.Serialization.cs#L226) (Azure SDK doesn\\u0026#39;t know it either) |\\n\\n`remote_function_call` is **not in the published [Foundry OpenAPI spec](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/tree/[REDACTED]/specification/ai-foundry/data-plane/Foundry/openapi3)** (`microsoft-foundry-openapi3.json` has `openapi_call` but no `remote_function_call`), yet the Conversations endpoint returns it. The [`AgentResponseItem` deserializer](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/src/Generated/Models/AgentResponseItem.Serialization.cs#L226) has a case for `openapi_call` but not `remote_function_call`, so Conversations items with tool calls fall through to `UnknownAgentResponseItem`.\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\n\\n1. **Stateless replay should work**: `OutputItems` from a Responses API call should be directly replayable as `InputItems` in a subsequent request without requiring `PreviousResponseId`. The server should accept `openapi_call` as input.\\n\\n2. **Consistent discriminator**: The same tool call should use the same `type` discriminator across both Responses and Conversations APIs, matching the published OpenAPI spec. `GetProjectConversationItemsAsync` should return typed `AgentResponseItem` subtypes (e.g. `OpenAPIToolCall`), not `UnknownAgentResponseItem`.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\n\\n1. Replaying `OutputItems` as `InputItems` → HTTP 400 rejecting `openapi_call` as input type.\\n2. Conversations API returns `remote_function_call` (undocumented) instead of `openapi_call`, which deserializes as `UnknownAgentResponseItem`.\\n\\n## Reproduction Steps\\n\\nMinimal repro for issue 1 (stateless replay failure):\\n\\n```csharp\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.AI.Projects;\\nusing Azure.AI.Projects.Agents;\\nusing Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI;\\nusing OpenAI.Responses;\\n\\nvar projectEndpoint = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\nvar modelDeploymentName = \\u0026#34;gpt-4o-mini\\u0026#34;;\\nAIProjectClient projectClient = new(new Uri(projectEndpoint), new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n// Create agent with OpenAPI tool\\nstring specPath = Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, \\u0026#34;weather_openapi.json\\u0026#34;);\\nOpenApiFunctionDefinition toolDef = new(\\n    name: \\u0026#34;get_weather\\u0026#34;,\\n    specificationBytes: BinaryData.FromBytes(File.ReadAllBytes(specPath)),\\n    authentication: new OpenAPIAnonymousAuthenticationDetails());\\nOpenAPITool openapiTool = new(toolDef);\\n\\nPromptAgentDefinition agentDef = new(model: modelDeploymentName)\\n{\\n    Instructions = \\u0026#34;You are a helpful assistant.\\u0026#34;,\\n    Tools = { openapiTool }\\n};\\nAgentVersion agentVersion = projectClient.Agents.CreateAgentVersion(\\u0026#34;myAgent\\u0026#34;, new(agentDef));\\n\\nProjectResponsesClient responseClient = projectClient.OpenAI.GetProjectResponsesClientForAgent(\\n    new AgentReference(agentVersion.Name, agentVersion.Version));\\n\\n// First request works\\nResponseResult response = responseClient.CreateResponse(\\u0026#34;What is the weather in Seattle?\\u0026#34;);\\nConsole.WriteLine(response.GetOutputText());\\n\\n// Stateless follow-up: replay OutputItems as InputItems → HTTP 400\\nvar opts = new CreateResponseOptions();\\nopts.InputItems.Add(ResponseItem.CreateUserMessageItem(\\u0026#34;What is the weather in Seattle?\\u0026#34;));\\nforeach (var item in response.OutputItems)\\n    opts.InputItems.Add(item);  // Contains openapi_call items\\nopts.InputItems.Add(ResponseItem.CreateUserMessageItem(\\u0026#34;What about Portland?\\u0026#34;));\\nResponseResult followUp = responseClient.CreateResponse(opts); // THROWS: HTTP 400\\n\\n// Cleanup\\nprojectClient.Agents.DeleteAgentVersion(agentVersion.Name, agentVersion.Version);\\n```\\n\\nMinimal repro for issue 2 (dual discriminator — `remote_function_call` instead of `openapi_call`):\\n\\n```csharp\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.AI.Projects;\\nusing Azure.AI.Projects.Agents;\\nusing Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI;\\nusing OpenAI.Responses;\\n\\nvar projectEndpoint = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\nvar modelDeploymentName = \\u0026#34;gpt-4o-mini\\u0026#34;;\\nAIProjectClient projectClient = new(new Uri(projectEndpoint), new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n// Create agent with OpenAPI tool (same setup as issue 1)\\nstring specPath = Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, \\u0026#34;weather_openapi.json\\u0026#34;);\\nOpenApiFunctionDefinition toolDef = new(\\n    name: \\u0026#34;get_weather\\u0026#34;,\\n    specificationBytes: BinaryData.FromBytes(File.ReadAllBytes(specPath)),\\n    authentication: new OpenAPIAnonymousAuthenticationDetails());\\nOpenAPITool openapiTool = new(toolDef);\\n\\nPromptAgentDefinition agentDef = new(model: modelDeploymentName)\\n{\\n    Instructions = \\u0026#34;You are a helpful assistant.\\u0026#34;,\\n    Tools = { openapiTool }\\n};\\nAgentVersion agentVersion = projectClient.Agents.CreateAgentVersion(\\n    agentName: \\u0026#34;myAgent\\u0026#34;, options: new AgentVersionCreationOptions(agentDef));\\n\\n// Create a conversation and send a query via Responses API\\nvar conversationsClient = projectClient.OpenAI.GetProjectConversationsClient();\\nProjectConversation conversation = await conversationsClient.CreateProjectConversationAsync();\\n\\nProjectResponsesClient responseClient = projectClient.OpenAI.GetProjectResponsesClientForAgent(\\n    new AgentReference(agentVersion.Name, agentVersion.Version));\\nCreateResponseOptions opts = new()\\n{\\n    InputItems = { ResponseItem.CreateUserMessageItem(\\u0026#34;What is the weather in Seattle?\\u0026#34;) },\\n};\\nopts.AgentConversationId = conversation.Id;\\nResponseResult response = responseClient.CreateResponse(opts);\\n\\n// Retrieve conversation items — tool calls come back as \\u0026#34;remote_function_call\\u0026#34;\\nawait foreach (AgentResponseItem item in conversationsClient.GetProjectConversationItemsAsync(conversation.Id))\\n{\\n    // All items are UnknownAgentResponseItem — none resolve to OpenApiToolCall\\n    // because the server returns \\u0026#34;type\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;remote_function_call\\u0026#34; (not \\u0026#34;openapi_call\\u0026#34;)\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;{item.GetType().Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n    // Prints: UnknownAgentResponseItem (×4)\\n}\\n\\n// Cleanup\\nawait conversationsClient.DeleteConversationAsync(conversation.Id);\\nprojectClient.Agents.DeleteAgentVersion(agentVersion.Name, agentVersion.Version);\\n```\\n\\n## Environment\\n\\n- Windows 11, .NET 10.0\\n- Azure.AI.Projects 2.0.0-beta.2\\n- Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI 2.0.0-beta.1 (transitive)\\n- OpenAI 2.9.1 (transitive)\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57684\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"jozkee\",\"id\":16040868,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/jozkee\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/16040868?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-01T21:41:30Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-01T21:41:30Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-01T21:45:27.9453478Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-01T21:43:44.687603413Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":57684,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-01T21:45:27.9494617Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-01T21:43:44.912997268Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57684,\"title\":\"New foundry *_call/*_call_output items can\\u0026#39;t be sent as Responses input; Conversations API returns undocumented remote_function_call instead\",\"body\":\"# [BUG] Foundry agent tool call types have dual discriminator and block stateless multi-turn replay\\n\\n## Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.AI.Projects 2.0.0-beta.2 (transitively pulls Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI 2.0.0-beta.1, OpenAI 2.9.1)\\n\\n## Describe the bug\\n\\nTwo related issues with how Azure-specific agent tool call types (e.g. `openapi_call`) surface through the Responses and Conversations APIs:\\n\\n### 1. Stateless multi-turn replay fails for Azure tool types\\n\\nWhen using `ProjectResponsesClient` with an agent that has Azure-specific tools (OpenAPI, AI Search, Bing Grounding), `ResponseResult.OutputItems` contains items with `\\u0026#34;type\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;openapi_call\\u0026#34;`. Attempting to replay these as `InputItems` in a follow-up request (stateless multi-turn) results in HTTP 400:\\n\\n```\\nHTTP 400 (invalid_request_error: invalid_value)\\nParameter: input[1]\\nInvalid value: \\u0026#39;openapi_call\\u0026#39;. Supported values are: \\u0026#39;apply_patch_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;apply_patch_call_output\\u0026#39;,\\n\\u0026#39;code_interpreter_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;compaction\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;computer_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;computer_call_output\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;custom_tool_call\\u0026#39;,\\n\\u0026#39;custom_tool_call_output\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;file_search_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;function_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;function_call_output\\u0026#39;,\\n\\u0026#39;image_generation_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;item_reference\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;local_shell_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;local_shell_call_output\\u0026#39;,\\n\\u0026#39;mcp_approval_request\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;mcp_approval_response\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;mcp_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;mcp_list_tools\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;message\\u0026#39;,\\n\\u0026#39;reasoning\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;shell_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;shell_call_output\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;tool_search_call\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;tool_search_output\\u0026#39;,\\nand \\u0026#39;web_search_call\\u0026#39;.\\n```\\n\\nThis forces users into server-managed state (`PreviousResponseId` or Conversations API) — there\\u0026#39;s no way to do stateless multi-turn with Azure-specific tools.\\n\\n### 2. Dual discriminator: Conversations API returns undocumented type\\n\\nThe same logical operation (an OpenAPI tool call) surfaces with different `type` values depending on the API:\\n\\n| API | JSON `type` value | SDK deserialization result |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Responses API `output[]` | `openapi_call` | [`InternalUnknownItemResource`](https://github.com/openai/openai-dotnet/blob/[REDACTED]/src/Generated/Models/Responses/ResponseItem.Serialization.cs#L158) (OpenAI SDK doesn\\u0026#39;t know it) |\\n| Conversations API items | `remote_function_call` (with `\\u0026#34;label\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;remote_openapi\\u0026#34;`) | [`UnknownAgentResponseItem`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/src/Generated/Models/AgentResponseItem.Serialization.cs#L226) (Azure SDK doesn\\u0026#39;t know it either) |\\n\\n`remote_function_call` is **not in the published [Foundry OpenAPI spec](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/tree/[REDACTED]/specification/ai-foundry/data-plane/Foundry/openapi3)** (`microsoft-foundry-openapi3.json` has `openapi_call` but no `remote_function_call`), yet the Conversations endpoint returns it. The [`AgentResponseItem` deserializer](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/src/Generated/Models/AgentResponseItem.Serialization.cs#L226) has a case for `openapi_call` but not `remote_function_call`, so Conversations items with tool calls fall through to `UnknownAgentResponseItem`.\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\n\\n1. **Stateless replay should work**: `OutputItems` from a Responses API call should be directly replayable as `InputItems` in a subsequent request without requiring `PreviousResponseId`. The server should accept `openapi_call` as input.\\n\\n2. **Consistent discriminator**: The same tool call should use the same `type` discriminator across both Responses and Conversations APIs, matching the published OpenAPI spec. `GetProjectConversationItemsAsync` should return typed `AgentResponseItem` subtypes (e.g. `OpenAPIToolCall`), not `UnknownAgentResponseItem`.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\n\\n1. Replaying `OutputItems` as `InputItems` → HTTP 400 rejecting `openapi_call` as input type.\\n2. Conversations API returns `remote_function_call` (undocumented) instead of `openapi_call`, which deserializes as `UnknownAgentResponseItem`.\\n\\n## Reproduction Steps\\n\\nMinimal repro for issue 1 (stateless replay failure):\\n\\n```csharp\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.AI.Projects;\\nusing Azure.AI.Projects.Agents;\\nusing Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI;\\nusing OpenAI.Responses;\\n\\nvar projectEndpoint = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\nvar modelDeploymentName = \\u0026#34;gpt-4o-mini\\u0026#34;;\\nAIProjectClient projectClient = new(new Uri(projectEndpoint), new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n// Create agent with OpenAPI tool\\nstring specPath = Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, \\u0026#34;weather_openapi.json\\u0026#34;);\\nOpenApiFunctionDefinition toolDef = new(\\n    name: \\u0026#34;get_weather\\u0026#34;,\\n    specificationBytes: BinaryData.FromBytes(File.ReadAllBytes(specPath)),\\n    authentication: new OpenAPIAnonymousAuthenticationDetails());\\nOpenAPITool openapiTool = new(toolDef);\\n\\nPromptAgentDefinition agentDef = new(model: modelDeploymentName)\\n{\\n    Instructions = \\u0026#34;You are a helpful assistant.\\u0026#34;,\\n    Tools = { openapiTool }\\n};\\nAgentVersion agentVersion = projectClient.Agents.CreateAgentVersion(\\u0026#34;myAgent\\u0026#34;, new(agentDef));\\n\\nProjectResponsesClient responseClient = projectClient.OpenAI.GetProjectResponsesClientForAgent(\\n    new AgentReference(agentVersion.Name, agentVersion.Version));\\n\\n// First request works\\nResponseResult response = responseClient.CreateResponse(\\u0026#34;What is the weather in Seattle?\\u0026#34;);\\nConsole.WriteLine(response.GetOutputText());\\n\\n// Stateless follow-up: replay OutputItems as InputItems → HTTP 400\\nvar opts = new CreateResponseOptions();\\nopts.InputItems.Add(ResponseItem.CreateUserMessageItem(\\u0026#34;What is the weather in Seattle?\\u0026#34;));\\nforeach (var item in response.OutputItems)\\n    opts.InputItems.Add(item);  // Contains openapi_call items\\nopts.InputItems.Add(ResponseItem.CreateUserMessageItem(\\u0026#34;What about Portland?\\u0026#34;));\\nResponseResult followUp = responseClient.CreateResponse(opts); // THROWS: HTTP 400\\n\\n// Cleanup\\nprojectClient.Agents.DeleteAgentVersion(agentVersion.Name, agentVersion.Version);\\n```\\n\\nMinimal repro for issue 2 (dual discriminator — `remote_function_call` instead of `openapi_call`):\\n\\n```csharp\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.AI.Projects;\\nusing Azure.AI.Projects.Agents;\\nusing Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI;\\nusing OpenAI.Responses;\\n\\nvar projectEndpoint = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\nvar modelDeploymentName = \\u0026#34;gpt-4o-mini\\u0026#34;;\\nAIProjectClient projectClient = new(new Uri(projectEndpoint), new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n// Create agent with OpenAPI tool (same setup as issue 1)\\nstring specPath = Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, \\u0026#34;weather_openapi.json\\u0026#34;);\\nOpenApiFunctionDefinition toolDef = new(\\n    name: \\u0026#34;get_weather\\u0026#34;,\\n    specificationBytes: BinaryData.FromBytes(File.ReadAllBytes(specPath)),\\n    authentication: new OpenAPIAnonymousAuthenticationDetails());\\nOpenAPITool openapiTool = new(toolDef);\\n\\nPromptAgentDefinition agentDef = new(model: modelDeploymentName)\\n{\\n    Instructions = \\u0026#34;You are a helpful assistant.\\u0026#34;,\\n    Tools = { openapiTool }\\n};\\nAgentVersion agentVersion = projectClient.Agents.CreateAgentVersion(\\n    agentName: \\u0026#34;myAgent\\u0026#34;, options: new AgentVersionCreationOptions(agentDef));\\n\\n// Create a conversation and send a query via Responses API\\nvar conversationsClient = projectClient.OpenAI.GetProjectConversationsClient();\\nProjectConversation conversation = await conversationsClient.CreateProjectConversationAsync();\\n\\nProjectResponsesClient responseClient = projectClient.OpenAI.GetProjectResponsesClientForAgent(\\n    new AgentReference(agentVersion.Name, agentVersion.Version));\\nCreateResponseOptions opts = new()\\n{\\n    InputItems = { ResponseItem.CreateUserMessageItem(\\u0026#34;What is the weather in Seattle?\\u0026#34;) },\\n};\\nopts.AgentConversationId = conversation.Id;\\nResponseResult response = responseClient.CreateResponse(opts);\\n\\n// Retrieve conversation items — tool calls come back as \\u0026#34;remote_function_call\\u0026#34;\\nawait foreach (AgentResponseItem item in conversationsClient.GetProjectConversationItemsAsync(conversation.Id))\\n{\\n    // All items are UnknownAgentResponseItem — none resolve to OpenApiToolCall\\n    // because the server returns \\u0026#34;type\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;remote_function_call\\u0026#34; (not \\u0026#34;openapi_call\\u0026#34;)\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;{item.GetType().Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n    // Prints: UnknownAgentResponseItem (×4)\\n}\\n\\n// Cleanup\\nawait conversationsClient.DeleteConversationAsync(conversation.Id);\\nprojectClient.Agents.DeleteAgentVersion(agentVersion.Name, agentVersion.Version);\\n```\\n\\n## Environment\\n\\n- Windows 11, .NET
